title: MELTS: 2025-2026 Attack Signal Overview
description: Correlates Metrics-style counts, Events, Logs, Traces, and Security state for the modern 2025-2026 demo attacks: ClickFix/CrashFix, SharePoint ToolShell, RMM abuse, AiTM token replay, and staged exfiltration.
query: ('Trace ID' = 'trace_clickfix_2026_001' or 'Trace ID' = 'trace_toolshell_sp_001' or 'Trace ID' = 'trace_rmm_2025_001' or 'Trace ID' = 'trace_aitm_token_2026_001' or 'Threat Name' = 'ClickFix Clipboard Execution' or 'Threat Name' = 'CrashFix Python RAT' or 'Threat Name' = 'RMM Tool Abuse' or 'Threat Name' = 'AiTM Token Replay' or 'Request URL' like '*spinstall0.aspx*' or 'Command Line' like '*ClickFix*' or 'Command Line' like '*CrashFix*' or 'Process Name' like '*ScreenConnect*' or 'Process Name' like '*AnyDesk*' or 'Process Name' like '*AteraAgent*' or 'User Name' = 'codeofconduct-reader@corp.example.com') | stats count as Events, unique('Host Name (Server)') as Hosts, unique('Source IP') as SourceIPs, unique('Destination IP') as Destinations by 'Log Source', 'Threat Name', 'Trace ID' | sort -Events

ask_ai_prompts: Summarize the 2025-2026 campaign by MELTS signal type and attack stage.
Which trace IDs and log sources provide the strongest evidence?

references.name: MELT observability pattern: Metrics, Events, Logs, and Traces

falsepositives: Authorized purple-team replay of the 2025-2026 demo campaign
